subject: Anthropology
title: Explanatory Models
description: Learn Explanatory Models in one minute.

hook.text: A doctor says bacteria. A grandmother says the evil eye. A patient says stress. Everyone explains sickness differently.

buildup.text: Explanatory models are the beliefs patients hold about what caused their illness, what it means, and how to fix it.

discovery.text: Kleinman showed that doctors and patients often hold conflicting models, leading to miscommunication and poor care.

twist.text: Effective healing happens when doctors listen to the patient's model, not just impose their own biomedical one.

climax.text: Healing is negotiation. The best care bridges the gap between what the doctor knows and what the patient believes.

punchline.text: Good medicine starts with asking: what do you think caused this?

quiz.question: What are explanatory models?
options: Patient beliefs about what caused their illness and how to treat it
